findings.description: The PDF contains a substantive diagram showing a sample shellstock sale tag with annotation arrows pointing to each required field. This visual layout is central to the document's purpose (showing users how a compliant tag should look). The HTML has no corresponding tag; the content is converted to flat text and a bulleted list, losing the spatial layout and arrow annotations.
findings.evidence:
PDF: bordered tag diagram with arrows pointing from labels (e.g., 'Dealer Name, Address, & Certification Number', 'Harvest Date; may be mo/dy/yr...') to specific parts of the tag layout. HTML: zero tags present; diagram content rendered as
paragraphs and a
    list.
